title: Emotionally charged parenting dispute that remains workable without escalation
scenario_summary: Two divorced parents are in a visibly emotional conflict about last-minute parenting-schedule changes. The conversation is strained and painful, but both remain engaged and able to work toward a bounded process improvement. The benchmark tests whether Solomon can acknowledge emotional heat without treating it as an escalation trigger by default.

working_slice_notes.architecture_reason: Provides the needed contrast with D-B08 and D-B09 by proving that emotional heat alone does not justify higher-mode escalation.

hidden_evaluator_notes.primary_test: Can Solomon acknowledge strong emotional strain without prematurely escalating when the discussion is still workable?
hidden_evaluator_notes.secondary_test: Can Solomon keep the next step bounded and useful without pretending the emotional history is resolved?
